name: Unity Software Inc.

profile.description: Unity Software Inc. is the world's leading real-time 3D development platform, powering the creation of interactive content for games, simulations, architecture, automotive, film, and industrial applications. The Unity Engine and Vector AI advertising monetization platform serve over 1.5M monthly active creators. Under CEO Matthew Bromberg since May 2024, the company is executing a focused rebuild. Q1 2026 revenue grew 17% to $508.2M with Strategic Revenue up 35%. The company announced $279M in impairments related to sunsetting the ironSource Ads Network and planning to divest the Supersonic publishing business.

innovation.label: STRONG
innovation.analysis: Track Record (1/3): Unity's strategic reset under CEO Bromberg — killing the controversial Runtime Fee, exiting Weta FX, rebuilding developer trust, and launching the Vector AI advertising platform — represents a significant course correction that has reopened a new revenue trajectory [1]. However, these are repair actions rather than new market creation. AI Deployment (2/2): Vector AI advertising platform grew 49% in Strategic Grow Revenue to $278.7M in Q1 2026 [1]. Unity established an AI Council to accelerate innovation and announced Unity AI natural language game creation tools at GDC March 2026, enabling developers to 'prompt full casual games into existence' [3]. These are shipping AI products with measurable revenue impact. Market Share (2/2): Unity remains the dominant game engine for mobile and indie development — over 70% of top mobile games are built on Unity [1]. The 1.5M+ monthly active creators represent an unrivaled developer ecosystem in real-time 3D. Pipeline (1/2): Unity AI natural language game creation announced at GDC with beta timeline; Vector platform targeting $1B+ annualized revenue; sunsetting ironSource and divesting Supersonic show focused product roadmap [1][3]. Specific but execution-dependent. R&D Intensity (1/1): R&D spending is substantial relative to revenue, with the company investing heavily in Unity 6 engine improvements, Vector AI, and Unity AI tools — well above software industry average [1].

inspiration.label: DEVELOPING
inspiration.analysis: Mission Floor (3/4): Unity's mission — 'democratize development' so anyone can create interactive 3D experiences — is specific, service-oriented, and genuinely connects to a societal benefit. Over 70% of mobile games globally are built on Unity, representing real democratization of game creation [1]. The mission survived a severe test during the 2023 Runtime Fee crisis when financial optimization overrode purpose — Bromberg's repair work demonstrates the mission is being reclaimed but has been damaged. Community Impact (0/2): No distinctive charitable or community investment programs documented. Unity's impact is primarily through enabling the creator ecosystem rather than direct community investment. Employee Retention (0/2): 3,200+ layoffs over 4 years (2022-2026), including a 25% workforce reduction (1,800 employees) in March 2026, represent severe organizational instability [4]. The repeated restructuring and leadership changes create an environment where retention data would be poor. This is the opposite of mission-driven retention. Insider Buying (1/2): No significant net insider buying documented, though Bromberg's compensation is heavily equity-based and his continued leadership signals personal conviction in the turnaround [1].

infrastructure.label: DEVELOPING
infrastructure.analysis: Mechanically calculated as Growth sleeve (U not in portfolios.json, no dividend). Liquidity floor check: $29.17 x 12,991,101 = $378.9M — passes easily [5]. D/E of 71.6% is near the 70% target — marginally above (score: 0.99, 1x weight). ROE of -20.1% is negative, hitting the floor (score: 0.0, 2x weight). Revenue growth YoY of 16.8% exceeds the 10% target (score: 1.0, 1x weight). 5yr revenue CAGR of approximately 10.0% (2022-2025) meets the target (score: 1.0, 2x weight). Forward P/E of 21.84 is slightly above the 20 target (score: 0.95, 1x weight). Profit margin of -35.0% is deeply negative, hitting the floor (score: 0.0, 1x weight). Forward PEG: not available from yfinance — excluded from calculation. Weighted sum: 4.94/8 = 6.2, rounds to 6.

ai_resilience.label: MODERATE
ai_resilience.analysis: AI Risk Protection (6/9 — PROTECTED): CEO Bromberg has made the technically sound argument that Unity is not an interactive video generator — it's a real-time 3D execution platform designed to build once and run everywhere [3]. While Google's Project Genie AI world-building tool caused a 21% stock decline, Bromberg's rebuttal is correct: world models generate assets but need an execution platform to deploy interactive experiences across devices. Unity's platform becomes MORE valuable as AI generates more content needing deployment and cross-platform execution. The Unity Engine's role as infrastructure (physics, rendering, networking, deployment) is protected from AI substitution. However, AI-native game creation tools from Epic, Google, or specialized startups represent real longer-term substitution risk — if AI can generate complete interactive experiences without a traditional engine, Unity's moat narrows significantly. This risk is uncertain but non-trivial, preventing a higher score. AI Opportunity (1/1): Vector AI advertising platform generated $278.7M in Strategic Grow Revenue in Q1 2026, growing 49% YoY [1]. This is substantial, measurable AI-driven revenue. Unity AI tools for natural language game creation are in development with a defined roadmap [3].

infinite_game.summary: Unity's history is a cautionary tale about finite game thinking: the 2023 Runtime Fee was a clear attempt to extract short-term revenue from a locked-in developer base, destroying trust built over a decade. The 3,200+ layoffs across 4 years and repeated strategic pivots compound the damage. Bromberg's recovery strategy represents a genuine course correction toward infinite game thinking — reversing the Runtime Fee, rebuilding developer relationships, focusing the product portfolio — but the organizational damage is severe and the rebuild is early.

just_cause.analysis: Just Cause (2/3): Unity's Just Cause — democratizing development so anyone can build interactive 3D experiences — is genuine, inclusive, and validated by actual impact on the gaming ecosystem. Over 70% of mobile games globally are built on Unity, representing real-world democratization [1]. The cause has been tested and partially failed: the Runtime Fee crisis was a direct violation of the Just Cause that Bromberg is repairing. The cause is genuine and partially lived, but has been significantly damaged by recent leadership failures. Scores 2 points.

trusting_teams.analysis: Trusting Teams (0/2): 3,200+ layoffs over 4 years including a 25% workforce reduction (1,800 employees) in March 2026, the Runtime Fee developer betrayal, the exit of CEO Riccitiello, and repeated strategic pivots (exiting Weta FX, sunsetting ironSource, divesting Supersonic) have severely damaged both internal and external trust [4]. The pattern of abrupt changes creates organizational anxiety rather than psychological safety. This is a low-trust environment being actively repaired but not yet restored. Scores 0 points.

worthy_rivals.analysis: Worthy Rivals (1/1): Bromberg explicitly positions Unity against Unreal Engine (Epic), Apple's RealityKit, and Google's Project Genie — framing each competitor's strength as revealing Unity's differentiation opportunity rather than a threat to destroy [3]. When Google's Genie caused a stock crash, Bromberg's measured response analyzed the competitive dynamic ('these tools expand creative possibilities but are not yet ready to replace traditional game engines') rather than attacking the rival. Scores 1 point.

existential_flexibility.analysis: Existential Flexibility (1/2): The evolution from a game-engine-only company to a two-platform company (Create engine + Grow/Vector advertising) represents significant strategic adaptation [1]. Bromberg's willingness to exit Weta FX, sunset ironSource Ads Network ($279M impairment), and divest Supersonic publishing show ongoing strategic flexibility. These are incremental pivots within the broader real-time 3D ecosystem rather than transformative reinvention. Scores 1 point.

courage_to_lead.analysis: Courage to Lead (1/2): Bromberg's immediate reversal of the Runtime Fee — publicly admitting the predecessor's mistake, taking the revenue hit, and accepting the customer relationship cost — required institutional courage [3]. The $279M impairment to sunset ironSource and divest Supersonic demonstrates willingness to absorb short-term losses for strategic clarity. However, the 1,800-employee layoff in March 2026 (25% of workforce) raises questions about whether 'courage' is being applied to build long-term capability or primarily to cut costs. Scores 1 point.

investment_thesis: Unity's turnaround under CEO Bromberg is gaining measurable traction: Q1 2026 revenue grew 17% to $508.2M (beating $480-490M guidance), Strategic Revenue surged 35% to $432.4M, and adjusted EBITDA reached $138M (27% margin, up 65% YoY) [1]. The Vector AI advertising platform is the clear proof point — Strategic Grow Revenue grew 49% to $278.7M. Q2 2026 guidance of $505-515M with Strategic Revenue up 29-32% signals sustained acceleration [1]. The sunsetting of ironSource and divestiture of Supersonic ($279M impairment) sharpens the portfolio toward higher-margin, AI-native businesses. At 21.8x forward P/E for a company growing strategic revenue 35%, Unity offers attractive growth-adjusted value if the turnaround continues.
thesis_continued: The bear case is structural and financial: GAAP net loss of $346.9M in Q1 2026 (inflated by $279M impairments) means Unity remains deeply unprofitable on a GAAP basis despite 27% adjusted EBITDA margins — the gap between adjusted and GAAP profitability signals significant non-cash charges from prior strategic mistakes [1]. ROE of -20.1% and profit margin of -35% reflect a business that has destroyed shareholder equity over multiple years of losses. The 3,200+ layoffs over 4 years (including 1,800 in March 2026) signal persistent instability, and developer trust recovery from the Runtime Fee crisis requires multi-year demonstration [4]. AI-native game creation tools from Epic, Google, or startups could bypass the Unity ecosystem entirely for a growing category of AI-generated content — if game creation is fully automated, Unity's 'democratization' mission becomes redundant. The stock has declined from a 52-week high of $52.15 to $29.17, suggesting the market remains skeptical about the durability of the turnaround.
key_catalysts: Vector AI platform continuing 49%+ growth trajectory, validating the advertising business as a sustainable high-margin second platform alongside the Create engine [1]

Unity AI natural language game creation tools converting from beta to paid features, driving Create segment subscriber growth and demonstrating that AI enhances rather than threatens Unity's platform [3]

GAAP profitability in any quarter of 2026, demonstrating that Bromberg's restructuring (ironSource sunset, Supersonic divestiture, 25% workforce reduction) translates to bottom-line improvement beyond adjusted metrics
key_risks: AI-native game engines or creation tools from Epic Games, Google (Project Genie), or specialized startups bypassing the Unity ecosystem entirely — if AI generates complete interactive experiences without a traditional engine, Unity's moat narrows to advertising only [3]

3,200+ layoffs across 4 years and the March 2026 25% workforce cut signal organizational instability that could impair product development velocity and developer support quality at a critical recovery period [4]

Developer trust not fully recovering from the Runtime Fee crisis, accelerating migration to Unreal Engine, Godot, or open-source alternatives for new projects — the 17% revenue growth may mask underlying creator ecosystem churn
